techniques.name: PassWalk Authentication
techniques.description: A multi-factor authentication technique that combines a textual password with active spatial interaction on a mobile mixed-reality headset. The user enters their password by selecting dynamically randomized characters using head-gaze while performing system-directed lateral foot movements, producing a time-constrained, spatial Authenticator Output that enhances resistance to observation attacks.
techniques.requirements: Mixed-reality headset with head-gaze tracking and foot-tracking capabilities
sources: @inproceedings{10.1145/3503161.3548252, author = {Kumar, Abhishek and Lee, Lik-Hang and Chauhan, Jagmohan and Su, Xiang and Hoque, Mohammad A. and Pirttikangas, Susanna and Tarkoma, Sasu and Hui, Pan}, title = {PassWalk: Spatial Authentication Leveraging Lateral Shift and Gaze on Mobile Headsets}, year = {2022}, isbn = {9781450392037}, publisher = {Association for Computing Machinery}, address = {New York, NY, USA}, url = {https://doi.org/10.1145/3503161.3548252}, doi = {10.1145/3503161.3548252}, booktitle = {Proceedings of the 30th ACM International Conference on Multimedia}, pages = {952–960}, numpages = {9}, ation = {Lisboa, Portugal}, series = {MM '22}}
